questions.question: Can you provide a specific example of a query you optimized in PostgreSQL, detailing the changes you made and the results you achieved?

transcripts.transcript: I have hands-on experience with explain, experience. In my recent backend work I personally owned implementation, troubleshooting, and optimization. For PostgreSQL and API performance, I started by identifying the failure mode, adding instrumentation, reviewing query plans, and using indexes, batching, and caching to remove bottlenecks. In one migration I reduced p95 latency by 38 percent, cut timeout incidents by more than half, and validated the rollout with load tests, alerts, and post-deploy metrics.

For PostgreSQL and API performance, I identified failure modes, added instrumentation, reviewed query plans, and used indexes, batching, and caching to remove bottlenecks. In one migration, I reduced p95 latency by 38 percent, cut timeout incidents by more than half, and validated the rollout with load tests, alerts, and post-deploy metrics.
